description_long: This is a small, single-engine airplane with a high-wing configuration and a fixed tricycle landing gear. The fuselage is streamlined and painted primarily white, with a subtle dark stripe running along the side. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and features a green-tinted windshield, suggesting an enclosed, single or dual-seat arrangement. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with a small tailwheel visible. The propeller is mounted at the nose, indicating a piston engine. The overall design is typical of light general aviation aircraft, suitable for short-range flights and training purposes.
description: A small, white, high-wing airplane with a single nose-mounted propeller, fixed tricycle landing gear, and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: The left side of the airplane is visible, showing the high-mounted wing, nose propeller, and main landing gear.
description_view_1: The rear-left view highlights the tail section, including the vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the tailwheel.
description_view_2: The right-front view displays the propeller, cockpit windshield, and both main landing gear wheels.
description_view_3: The front view emphasizes the nose propeller, cockpit, and the full wingspan.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral angle and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a green-tinted windshield, likely single or dual-seat.
